entries.definition:
dh–ʾ–m to expel, to drive out; to insult, to compel, to humiliate, a fault. Of this root, only مَذْءُوم madhʾūm occurs once in the Qur’an.
مَذْءُوم madhʾūm [pass. part.] humiliated and despised (7:18) قَالَ اخْرُجْ مِنْهَا مَذْءُومًا مَّدْحُورًا He said, ‘Get out!, you are disgraced and banished!’

مَذْءُوم madhʾūm [pass. part.] humiliated and despised (7:18) قَالَ اخْرُجْ مِنْهَا مَذْءُومًا مَّدْحُورًا He said, ‘Get out!, you are disgraced and banished!’
